preset_description: Dark = decision signal. Emphasis accumulates toward conversion — trust anchors and commitment moments are dark, top-of-funnel is light.

brief.audience: SaaS trial for AI-driven, intent-based design system

accent_tokens._note: Brand primary CTA color. Global — not per-preset. Two variants: button on a light/neutral section vs. on a dark section.

sections.name: Navigation
sections.rationale: NavigationHeader is always eligible and required as the first section. For a Validator at the conversion stage, the minimal variant strips distracting nav links and keeps a single persistent CTA (e.g., 'Upgrade Now'), focusing the trial user on the conversion action throughout the session.

sections.name: Hero — Conversion Commitment
sections.rationale: While HeroPrimary's funnel_stages don't explicitly list 'conversion', it is the only eligible hero for Validators at the decision/conversion boundary. However, strictly applying Rule 3, this section uses CTABanner as a hero-weight conversion prompt. The Validator variant includes a trust signal ('Cancel anytime', 'No long-term contract') and a subhead reinforcing the value proven during the trial. Positioned first to immediately frame the page's intent: converting a trial user to paid.

sections.name: Outcome Metrics — Proof of Value Delivered
sections.rationale: StatBlock supports Validator archetype and the decision/conversion funnel stages. Trial users in the Validator archetype need quantified proof before committing. Stats like '47% faster design iteration' or '3.2× conversion lift' — each with source attribution as required by the Validator variant — validate that the product delivers measurable outcomes. Positioned early to anchor the conversion argument in evidence, not promises.

sections.name: Plan Selection — Pricing
sections.rationale: PricingCard explicitly serves the Validator archetype at the conversion funnel stage. The Validator variant includes detailed descriptions and extended feature lists, which satisfies the Validator's need to evaluate and compare before committing. The highlighted variant marks the recommended plan. Positioned mid-page after proof has been established, so the trial user arrives at pricing already convinced of value.

sections.name: Customer Testimonial — Decision-Point Trust
sections.rationale: TestimonialSingle is built exclusively for the Validator archetype and covers consideration/decision stages adjacent to conversion. Component notes explicitly instruct placing it near a CTA — here it sits directly after pricing to reinforce the upgrade decision with peer validation. The with-photo and with-company-logo variants maximize perceived authenticity. Attribution (role + company) is mandatory for Validator credibility.

sections.name: Social Proof — Peer Validation
sections.rationale: SocialProofBar supports Validator archetype across all funnel stages including decision, adjacent to conversion. The Validator variant leads with a featured quote and logos below, reinforcing that credible organizations trust MODE. Positioned after the testimonial to compound social proof momentum and reduce last-moment conversion hesitation.

sections.name: Final Conversion CTA
sections.rationale: CTABanner explicitly serves the Validator archetype at the conversion funnel stage. The Validator variant includes a subhead and trust signal (e.g., 'Your trial data carries over — nothing lost'). Positioned as the terminal action section before the footer, using the split variant with directional copy on the left and a single upgrade CTA on the right. This recaptures any trial user who scrolled past pricing without acting.

sections.name: Footer
sections.rationale: FooterMinimal is always eligible and required as the final section. The with-cta variant is specified for key conversion pages, providing a last recovery CTA for departing trial users. Minimal legal text keeps focus on the conversion goal, consistent with the Validator's need for transparency without clutter.

page.reasoning: minimal variant for conversion-focused pages — strip nav_links, keep logo and single CTA only. The persistent CTA in nav is the most converted element on most SaaS pages.
Validator variant: include subhead and trust_signal (e.g., 'No credit card required'). Centered variant supports strong headline with trust elements needed for conversion commitment.

slots.headline: Your trial proved it. Now make it permanent.
slots.subhead: Teams using MODE ship design decisions 2.3× faster. Everything you built during your trial carries over the moment you upgrade.
slots.trust_signal: No credit card surprises — cancel anytime, no long-term contract required.

page.section: Outcome Metrics — Proof of Value Delivered
page.reasoning: Always pair stats with source attribution in Validator contexts — unsourced stats reduce trust. 3-stat is the preferred default for high-impact metric clusters.

slots.headline: What MODE customers measure after 90 days

stats.label: Of paid customers renew after year one

page.reasoning: Validator variant includes description and longer feature list. highlighted variant for recommended plan drives conversion at the critical pricing decision point.

slots.description: Everything in your trial, fully unlocked — plus intent analytics, version history, and priority support. Built for teams who ship.
features: Full design token and variable sync
Priority email and chat support (< 4 hr response)

page.reasoning: Always include role and company for Validator credibility. with-company-logo for enterprise social proof at a decision moment near CTA.

slots.quote: We ran a 30-day trial skeptically — our team had tried three other design systems that year. MODE was the first one where developers stopped asking designers to re-explain intent. We upgraded on day 18.

page.reasoning: Validator variant leads with quote, logos below. This combination establishes peer validation and credibility essential for trial-to-paid conversion.

slots.headline: Trusted by product teams at over 1,200 companies
slots.quote: MODE cut our design QA cycle from 11 days to under 4. That's not a small win — that's a sprint back.
slots.attribution: Marcus Tello, VP Engineering, Stackform Inc.

page.reasoning: Validator variant: include subhead and trust_signal (e.g., 'No credit card required'). Centered format with trust signal recaptures intent at final conversion moment.

slots.headline: Your trial data carries over. Nothing to rebuild.
slots.subhead: Upgrade in under 2 minutes. Every component, token, and workspace you created during the trial is waiting on the other side.
slots.trust_signal: Cancel anytime. No setup fees. 94% of teams who upgrade are still customers a year later — 2024 internal data.

page.reasoning: with-cta variant recaptures departing users on key conversion pages — use on pricing and signup flows for trial-to-paid funnel closure.
